channel.title: Random Comics
channel.description: Standalone comics, strange experiments, one-shot adventures, spontaneous comic ideas, and future comic series.

item.title: Past Due Miracle
item.description: After Second Skin fails against the Immortal Curse, Adam Frankenstein takes God's first repo job and reclaims stolen Lucid Light from a fake miracle preacher. Issue #1 of Divine Repo. 9 pages.

item.title: The Map That Feared the Ending
item.description: An anxious young mapmaker dreams into Inkfall Reef and must help a Dream Guardian trap a Nightmare that feeds on her fear of choosing the wrong ending. Issue #1 of Storyverse Comics. 8 pages.

item.title: Captain Galaxy: The Living Castle
item.description: When the living Cosmic Castle reveals that Captain Galaxy has only ever been its disposable host, the frightened hero must decide whether to accept the doomed Storyverse or prove his courage by shielding every story from a universe-ending flood. Standalone issue. 8 pages.

item.title: Channel Zero
item.description: A midnight lightning strike bonds local access technician Bob Carlson to a cosmic remote, forcing him to cast forgotten story-forms against a Nightmare static kaiju. Issue #1 of Dream Caster. 8 pages.

item.title: Backrooms Lucid Light
item.description: Lucid dreamer Jet Black falls through his own couch into the Backrooms, loses access to his purple Lucid Light, and survives a hunting mimic crate by turning the Backrooms' no-clipping logic against itself. Standalone issue. 9 pages.

item.title: Nothing Special
item.description: Rejected at birth for having no Hacks, Genny Manson turns the world's most ignored power into the first proof that he can become a hero. Issue #1 of Generic Man. 8 pages.

item.title: Spark of Lucid Light
item.description: After a global event called the Spark gives some people fantastic abilities and makes them targets, Jet Black discovers his dream power is real when a government capture robot tears him from his apartment and forces him to choose between hiding and joining the resistance. Standalone issue. 8 pages.

item.title: Anamorphis UAP
item.description: A drained comic-book creator sees a blurry leaked military video of a luminous faceless being and chooses to turn that disputed evidence into art that helps people look for wonder again. Standalone issue. 8 pages.

item.title: The Battery Problem
item.description: Dark Light's one-soldier-at-a-time hunt across the Storyverse exposes the difference between temporary Lucid Dreamers and rare native Lucid Conduits, turning King Kaine Chester into an unavoidable target. Issue #3 of The Three Hands. 8 pages.

item.title: The King's Three Hands
item.description: Kaine tests his kingdom's strongest defenders against hardlight Anamorphis simulations and learns that the right Three Hands must be knights who can harm light, keep choosing the fight, and still trust the king he once was. Issue #4 of The Three Hands. 8 pages.

item.title: The Limits of Lucid Light
item.description: Before warning Kaine's island city, the Lucid Lord returns to his rigid homeworld to prove his worth, only to discover that even Lucid Light cannot rewrite a story already locked under Dark Light's rule. Issue #2 of The Three Hands. 8 pages.

item.title: The Promise of Lucid Light
item.description: King Kaine Chester's island city nears rebellion under his corrupted Lucid Light rule until the Lucid Lord cleanses him and warns that a greater darkness is coming for his power. Issue #1 of The Three Hands. 8 pages.

item.title: N-7: The Hoax File
item.description: When Anonymous releases a buried 1994 contact file about N-7, a non-human feline humanoid recovered at the Nevada Test and Training Range, the world calls it a hoax. The leak argues that the hoax story was built to hide the only witness who told the truth. Standalone issue. 8 pages.

item.title: The Dark Above
item.description: After a late superhero movie, a wealthy family's shortcut through a neglected alley becomes an origin story for something predatory when their young son discovers flight, fangs, and a hunger that spreads. Standalone issue. 8 pages.

item.title: The Section 10 Files
item.description: After UFO disclosure files coincide with a wave of vanished scientists, an Anonymous cell digs through nearly 200 releases and discovers the disappearances point to shapeshifting "Dark Clothed Creatures," forcing the hackers to seek psychic contact with Master Kalen-Li and the hidden Korrendorian guardians of Earth. Standalone issue. 8 pages.

item.title: Bun-Bun and the Lightless God
item.description: When ash from a volcanic eruption starves a stranded Valordian crew of sunlight, their youngest explorer steals sacred ore from a cave god's shrine and survives only by turning her flight field into a desperate act of escape. Standalone issue. 8 pages.

item.title: Figure Fiddler: Dust Demon
item.description: A blocked writer named Toby slips into an action-figure daydream where his cluttered office becomes a besieged toy city, and only a holy hoverbike built from a handheld vacuum can save his corrupted collection from a Dust Demon. Standalone issue. 8 pages.

item.title: Particle Patriot: Time Displacement
item.description: On an early government mission, a quantum superhero made of red, white, and blue atoms must escape the Jurassic past and a ruined future to stop Clocktor before one stolen moment becomes a permanent empire. Standalone issue. 8 pages.

item.title: Deca-Form: Island Retirement
item.description: After a supernatural storm destroys his cruise ship, Deca-Form must stop treating survival as a rescue mission and use his alien transformations to build a new life on an empty island. Standalone issue. 9 pages.
